revision.comment: actually just point that section at classes
revision.text: {{update|reason=Thought in [[Special:Diff/136610358|June 2007]] to be outdated, and all these years later this page has received virtually no updated content|discuss=Not a lot of new content}} {{Wikipedia how to}} This page documents various CSS elements that are useful to know when working in the article and template namespaces. For information about how to use them, see: == Classes == {{main|Wikipedia:Catalogue of CSS classes}} == Specific styles == *'''border-collapse:collapse''' This style will eliminate 3-d style borders created by the border attribute and collapse them in 1px wide borders. ''This property will override cellspacing!'' To define similar whitespace, use the border-spacing property (although it is not possible to have both border-spacing and border-collapse for the same table). ===Moving stuff=== *'''float:right''' and '''clear:right''' These styles are used to move a table to the right-hand side of the screen. Float right is equivalent to align="right" The clear code property makes it impossible for the movement to be blocked by another box or picture: the template will automatically move under it. ===Margins and sizes=== *'''margin:0 0 0.5em 1em;''' This style prevents text from running straight to the template by saving blank space. It should be used on boxes which are floated right (using '''float: right;'''). **'''margin:0 1em 0.5em 0;''' This performs the same function as the style directly above, but should be used for boxes which are floated left (using '''float: left;'''). ==See also== *[[Wikipedia:Catalogue of CSS classes]] *[[Wikipedia:WikiProject Microformats/classes|Classes used in microformats]] {{Wikipedia technical help|collapsed}} [[Category:Wikipedia template help|Styles]]

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: mass importing wikipedia pages
revision.text: {{update|reason=Thought in [[Special:Diff/136610358|June 2007]] to be outdated, and all these years later this page has received virtually no updated content|discuss=Not a lot of new content}} {{Wikipedia how to}} This page documents various CSS elements that are useful to know when working in the article and template namespaces. For information about how to use them, see: == Classes == {{main|Wikipedia:Catalogue of CSS classes}} == Specific styles == *'''border-collapse:collapse''' This style will eliminate 3-d style borders created by the border attribute and collapse them in 1px wide borders. ''This property will override cellspacing!'' To define similar whitespace, use the border-spacing property (although it is not possible to have both border-spacing and border-collapse for the same table). ===Moving stuff=== *'''float:right''' and '''clear:right''' These styles are used to move a table to the right-hand side of the screen. Float right is equivalent to align="right" The clear code property makes it impossible for the movement to be blocked by another box or picture: the template will automatically move under it. ===Margins and sizes=== *'''margin:0 0 0.5em 1em;''' This style prevents text from running straight to the template by saving blank space. It should be used on boxes which are floated right (using '''float: right;'''). **'''margin:0 1em 0.5em 0;''' This performs the same function as the style directly above, but should be used for boxes which are floated left (using '''float: left;'''). ==See also== *[[Wikipedia:Catalogue of CSS classes]] *[[Wikipedia:WikiProject Microformats/classes|Classes used in microformats]] {{Wikipedia technical help|collapsed}} [[Category:Wikipedia template help|Styles]]

{{update|reason=Thought in [[Special:Diff/136610358|June 2007]] to be outdated, and all these years later this page has received virtually no updated content|discuss=Not a lot of new content}} {{Wikipedia how to}} This page documents various CSS elements that are useful to know when working in the article and template namespaces. For information about how to use them, see: == Classes == {{main|Wikipedia:Catalogue of CSS classes}} == Specific styles == *'''border-collapse:collapse''' This style will eliminate 3-d style borders created by the border attribute and collapse them in 1px wide borders. ''This property will override cellspacing!'' To define similar whitespace, use the border-spacing property (although it is not possible to have both border-spacing and border-collapse for the same table). ===Moving stuff=== *'''float:right''' and '''clear:right''' These styles are used to move a table to the right-hand side of the screen. Float right is equivalent to align="right" The clear code property makes it impossible for the movement to be blocked by another box or picture: the template will automatically move under it. ===Margins and sizes=== *'''margin:0 0 0.5em 1em;''' This style prevents text from running straight to the template by saving blank space. It should be used on boxes which are floated right (using '''float: right;'''). **'''margin:0 1em 0.5em 0;''' This performs the same function as the style directly above, but should be used for boxes which are floated left (using '''float: left;'''). ==See also== *[[Wikipedia:Catalogue of CSS classes]] *[[Wikipedia:WikiProject Microformats/classes|Classes used in microformats]] {{Wikipedia technical help|collapsed}} [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects D]]

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Bot: remove deleted category Automatic wikipedia redirects D
revision.text: {{update|reason=Thought in [[Special:Diff/136610358|June 2007]] to be outdated, and all these years later this page has received virtually no updated content|discuss=Not a lot of new content}} {{Wikipedia how to}} This page documents various CSS elements that are useful to know when working in the article and template namespaces. For information about how to use them, see: == Classes == {{main|Wikipedia:Catalogue of CSS classes}} == Specific styles == *'''border-collapse:collapse''' This style will eliminate 3-d style borders created by the border attribute and collapse them in 1px wide borders. ''This property will override cellspacing!'' To define similar whitespace, use the border-spacing property (although it is not possible to have both border-spacing and border-collapse for the same table). ===Moving stuff=== *'''float:right''' and '''clear:right''' These styles are used to move a table to the right-hand side of the screen. Float right is equivalent to align="right" The clear code property makes it impossible for the movement to be blocked by another box or picture: the template will automatically move under it. ===Margins and sizes=== *'''margin:0 0 0.5em 1em;''' This style prevents text from running straight to the template by saving blank space. It should be used on boxes which are floated right (using '''float: right;'''). **'''margin:0 1em 0.5em 0;''' This performs the same function as the style directly above, but should be used for boxes which are floated left (using '''float: left;'''). ==See also== *[[Wikipedia:Catalogue of CSS classes]] *[[Wikipedia:WikiProject Microformats/classes|Classes used in microformats]] {{Wikipedia technical help|collapsed}}
